# Liveblocks Collaborative Form (Storage-backed Inputs)

## Background

You are extending a Next.js (App Router) starter that wires up a Liveblocks `RoomProvider` for a single shared collaborative form. The Storage root is intended to be a `LiveObject` with three text fields — `name`, `email`, `message` — plus a `lastUpdate` ISO timestamp. Every keystroke from any connected client must propagate to all other clients through Liveblocks Storage, and the page must show a small badge with the latest ISO timestamp of the most recent write.

The starter scaffolding already pre-installs Node 20, Next.js 14, and the Liveblocks v2 React + Node SDKs. The Liveblocks auth endpoint and `liveblocks.config.ts` types are in place. **The page itself is intentionally not yet wired to Storage** — it currently only renders the static inputs and you must complete the Storage integration.

The app MUST be wired to the real Liveblocks cloud (no mocks, no fake WebSocket, no in-memory stub).

## Requirements

* The collaborative form lives at the route `/` (the App Router root page). All connected clients must join the same Liveblocks room.
* Storage layout (must match exactly): `Storage.form: LiveObject<{ name: string; email: string; message: string; lastUpdate: string }>`.
* The page MUST be a Client Component (or wrap one) since Liveblocks hooks require the client runtime.
* The `RoomProvider` is declared with `initialStorage={{ form: new LiveObject({ name: "", email: "", message: "", lastUpdate: "" }) }}` so a first-visit room starts with empty fields.
* All three inputs must be **two-way bound** to Storage: their visible value is read from `useStorage((root) => root.form.<field>)`, and any user typing into the inputs must call a `useMutation` that writes the new value back to the same `LiveObject` field. Every write must also update `form.lastUpdate` to a fresh `new Date().toISOString()` value.
* The page must render a `<span id="form-last-update">` containing the current `lastUpdate` ISO string from Storage.
* All Liveblocks access must go through the official `@liveblocks/react` provider configured with a server-side authentication endpoint at `/api/liveblocks-auth` that uses `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` and `@liveblocks/node`. `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` may also be referenced.
* Use Liveblocks **v2** SDKs (`@liveblocks/client` and `@liveblocks/react` major version 2). Use `LiveObject` for Storage, not bare JS objects.
* The room id MUST be derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` at build/runtime. Read the run id from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable, re-export it as `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID` BEFORE running `npm run build` and `npm run start`, and use it inside the client as the room id: `form-storage-${NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID}`. (The verifier ALWAYS starts the server itself, so make sure you do **NOT** leave a development server running.)
* **You MUST kill any dev server** (`npm run dev`, `next dev`, background `npm run start`) that you started during implementation before signaling completion. The verifier launches its own production server on port `3000`; a leftover process will conflict with it.

## Implementation Hints

* Use `useStorage((root) => root.form.name)` (and equivalents) to read each field; use `useMutation` to write back into the `LiveObject` via `storage.get("form").set("name", value)`.
* A single `useMutation` that takes `(field, value)` is enough to update any of the three text fields plus `lastUpdate`.
* For Next.js App Router, wrap the page with a `"use client"` boundary; use `@liveblocks/react/suspense` and `ClientSideSuspense` while Storage loads.
* The Liveblocks v2 React SDK uses global types via `declare global { interface Liveblocks { Storage: ... } }`, which is already declared for you in `liveblocks.config.ts`.
* In the auth route, use `new Liveblocks({ secret }).prepareSession(...).allow(room, session.FULL_ACCESS)` and return the body from `session.authorize()` — this is the v2 server SDK API.
* Do **not** add `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` or `ZEALT_RUN_ID` to `liveblocks.config.ts`. The room id wiring belongs in the page / room provider.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: `/home/user/project`
* Start command: `npm run start` (after `npm run build`). The verifier sets `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID` before running both `build` and `start`.
* Port: `3000`
* Routes:
  * `GET /` returns the collaborative form HTML.
  * `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` returns HTTP 200 with a non-empty JSON body containing a `token` field minted by `@liveblocks/node` against the real Liveblocks API. Two consecutive calls must return different tokens.
* DOM contract (these are the only stable selectors used by verification):
  * `<input id="form-name">` — collaborative name text input.
  * `<input id="form-email">` — collaborative email text input.
  * `<textarea id="form-message">` — collaborative message textarea.
  * `<span id="form-last-update">` — shows the current `lastUpdate` ISO string from Storage. Empty string is allowed before the first edit.
* Behavior (verified across two browser contexts sharing the same room):
  * Any keystroke by context A in any field is reflected in the same field on context B within a few seconds.
  * The reverse holds: edits by B are seen by A.
  * After at least one edit, `#form-last-update` contains a value that parses as ISO-8601 (e.g. matches `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS(.sss)?Z` or equivalent ISO format).
  * When context A reloads the page after both clients disconnect, the `name`/`email`/`message` it sees on first paint match the last persisted values — i.e. Storage is durable.
* No mocks: `@liveblocks/client`, `@liveblocks/react`, and `@liveblocks/node` must remain real dependencies. There must be no `jest.mock(...)`/`vi.mock(...)` of any `@liveblocks/*` module anywhere under the project source.
* Real Liveblocks cloud only — no fake WebSocket, no shadowed packages under `node_modules/@liveblocks/*`.

Let me analyze this task carefully. I need to:

1. Explore the existing project structure
2. Understand what's already in place
3. Implement the collaborative form with Liveblocks Storage integration
4. Make sure everything works correctly

Let me start by exploring the project structure.

- The page MUST be a Client Component (or wrap one) since Liveblocks hooks require the client runtime
- The `RoomProvider` needs to be declared with `initialStorage={{ form: new LiveObject({ name: "", email: "", message: "", lastUpdate: "" }) }}`
- All three inputs must be two-way bound to Storage
- Use `useStorage` and `useMutation`
- The page must render `<span id="form-last-update">`

I think the cleanest approach is:
1. Keep `app/page.tsx` as a client component (it already has "use client")
2. Import and use `RoomProvider`, `useStorage`, `useMutation`, `ClientSideSuspense` from `@liveblocks/react/suspense`
3. Import `LiveObject` from `@liveblocks/client`
4. Derive the room id from `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID`

Wait, but `RoomProvider` needs to be a parent of the component that uses `useStorage` and `useMutation`. So I need to either:
- Have the page itself contain a `RoomProvider` wrapper and a separate inner component that uses the hooks
- Or split into two files

Let me create a clean structure:
- `app/page.tsx` - Client component with RoomProvider wrapping an inner FormComponent
- The FormComponent uses useStorage and useMutation

Actually, since the page already has "use client", I can put everything in one file. But having RoomProvider in the same component as useStorage/useMutation won't work because hooks need to be inside the provider.

So I'll need:
1. A `CollaborativeForm` inner component that uses `useStorage` and `useMutation`
2. The `Page` component that wraps it with `RoomProvider`

Let me write this out.

Also, I need to make sure the room id is derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID`. In Next.js, environment variables prefixed with `NEXT_PUBLIC_` are available on the client side via `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_*`.

The requirement says: "Read the run id from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable, re-export it as `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID` BEFORE running `npm run build` and `npm run start`"

So the verifier will set `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` before building. I just need to use `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` in the client code.

Let me also check if there's a `.env` file or similar.

Let me also make sure the auth route is correct. Looking at it again, it seems fine - it uses the v2 Node SDK API properly.

Now let me write the page.tsx:

part.text:
Now let me implement the page. I need to:

1. Create a `RoomProvider` wrapper with the correct room id and initial storage
2. Create an inner form component that uses `useStorage` and `useMutation`
3. Use `ClientSideSuspense` for loading state

Let me write the page.tsx now.

Now I'll implement the collaborative form page. The key changes:
- Replace local state with Liveblocks Storage (`useStorage`, `useMutation`)
- Wrap with `RoomProvider` using room id derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID`
- Use `ClientSideSuspense` for loading state
- Bind all inputs two-way to Storage

Now let me check if the Liveblocks provider needs to be configured. In Liveblocks v2, the `RoomProvider` needs a `client` prop or there needs to be a `LiveblocksProvider` somewhere. Let me check the Liveblocks v2 API.

In Liveblocks v2 React SDK, you need to wrap with `LiveblocksProvider` which takes a `publicKey` or custom `authEndpoint`. The `RoomProvider` is then nested inside.

Wait, actually looking at the Liveblocks v2 docs, the provider setup is:
1. `LiveblocksProvider` with `publicKey={NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY}` and optionally `authEndpoint="/api/liveblocks-auth"`
2. `RoomProvider` with `id={roomId}` and `initialStorage={...}`

But in some versions, `RoomProvider` can accept the auth config directly. Let me check the installed version.

The package.json shows `@liveblocks/react` version `2.20.0`. In v2, the typical setup is:

```tsx
import { LiveblocksProvider, RoomProvider } from "@liveblocks/react/suspense";

<LiveblocksProvider publicPk={publicKey} authEndpoint="/api/liveblocks-auth">
  <RoomProvider id={roomId} initialStorage={...}>
    ...
  </RoomProvider>
</LiveblocksProvider>
```

Or in v2, you can pass `publicKey` directly to `RoomProvider`... Actually, no, I think in v2 you need `LiveblocksProvider` as well.

Wait, let me look at the v2 docs more carefully. In Liveblocks v2:
- `LiveblocksProvider` is needed to configure the client (public key, auth endpoint, etc.)
- `RoomProvider` connects to a specific room

So I need to add `LiveblocksProvider` as well. Let me update the page.

Actually, looking at some examples, in v2 the `RoomProvider` can work standalone with `id`, `initialStorage`, etc. But the authentication configuration needs to be somewhere.

In v2, you can configure auth via `LiveblocksProvider`:

```tsx
<LiveblocksProvider publicPk={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY} authEndpoint="/api/liveblocks-auth">
  <RoomProvider id={roomId} initialStorage={...}>
    ...
  </RoomProvider>
</LiveblocksProvider>
```

Or you can use `createClient` to create a client and pass it to `RoomProvider`.

Let me check the actual API of the installed packages tobe sure.

Now I understand the Liveblocks v2 API. The `ClientOptions` (which `LiveblocksProvider` takes) requires either `publicApiKey` or `authEndpoint`. Since we have a custom auth endpoint at `/api/liveblocks-auth`, I should use `authEndpoint`.

Let me update the page.tsx to include `LiveblocksProvider` with `authEndpoint="/api/liveblocks-auth"`.

"All Liveblocks access must go through the official `@liveblocks/react` provider configured with a server-side authentication endpoint at `/api/liveblocks-auth` that uses `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` and `@liveblocks/node`. `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` may also be referenced."

So the auth endpoint is the primary mechanism. I should use `authEndpoint` in the `LiveblocksProvider`. But actually, in Liveblocks v2, the typical pattern for custom auth is:

```tsx
<LiveblocksProvider authEndpoint="/api/liveblocks-auth">
```

Or alternatively, you can use `publicApiKey` with `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY`.

Since we have a custom auth endpoint, I'll use `authEndpoint`. But actually, looking at the Liveblocks v2 docs, the `LiveblocksProvider` needs either `publicApiKey` or `authEndpoint`. Since we have a custom auth endpoint, I should use `authEndpoint`.

Actually wait - in Liveblocks v2, both `publicApiKey` and `authEndpoint` can be used. If `publicApiKey` is provided, it uses Liveblocks' built-in auth. If `authEndpoint` is provided, it uses the custom endpoint. Since we have a custom endpoint, I should use `authEndpoint`.

But actually, let me re-read the requirements more carefully. It says:

In the `suspense.d.ts`, the hooks like `useStorage` are the suspense versions (`_useStorageSuspense as aB`), while the ones in `index.d.ts` are the non-suspense versions (`useStorage as aj`).

The build succeeded. Now let me make sure there are no dev servers running and verify the auth endpoint works correctly.

Let me also check if there are any issues:

2. The page correctly uses `LiveblocksProvider` with `authEndpoint="/api/liveblocks-auth"`.
3. The room id is derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID`.
4. The `RoomProvider` has `initialStorage` set correctly.
5. The `CollaborativeForm` component uses `useStorage` and `useMutation` from `@liveblocks/react/suspense`.
6. The DOM contract is maintained with `id="form-name"`, `id="form-email"`, `id="form-message"`, and `id="form-last-update"`.

2. The `RoomProvider` needs to be inside a `LiveblocksProvider` - ✅ I have this set up correctly.

3. The `ClientSideSuspense` wraps the `CollaborativeForm` component - ✅ This is set up correctly.

4. The `useStorage` hooks read from `root.form.name`, `root.form.email`, etc. - ✅ This matches the Storage type.

5. The `useMutation` writes to `storage.get("form").set(field, value)` and updates `lastUpdate` - ✅ This is correct.

6. The `initialStorage` is `new LiveObject({ name: "", email: "", message: "", lastUpdate: "" })` - ✅ This matches the requirement.

input.result:
I've implemented the Liveblocks collaborative form with Storage-backed inputs. Here's a summary of the changes:

**`app/page.tsx`** — Replaced local React state with Liveblocks Storage integration:
- Added `LiveblocksProvider` with `authEndpoint="/api/liveblocks-auth"` to configure authentication
- Added `RoomProvider` with room id derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` (`form-storage-${NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID}`)
- Set `initialStorage={{ form: new LiveObject({ name: "", email: "", message: "", lastUpdate: "" }) }}` on `RoomProvider`
- Used `useStorage` (suspense version) to read each field from `root.form.name`, `root.form.email`, `root.form.message`, and `root.form.lastUpdate`
- Used `useMutation` to write field updates back to the `LiveObject`, updating both the changed field and `lastUpdate` with `new Date().toISOString()` in a single mutation
- Wrapped the form component in `ClientSideSuspense` with a loading fallback
- Maintained the DOM contract: `<input id="form-name">`, `<input id="form-email">`, `<textarea id="form-message">`, `<span id="form-last-update">`

**Unchanged files** (already correct):
- `app/api/liveblocks-auth/route.ts` — Uses Liveblocks v2 Node SDK (`new Liveblocks({ secret }).prepareSession(...).allow(room, session.FULL_ACCESS)`)
- `liveblocks.config.ts` — Declares the `Storage` type with `form: LiveObject<{ name, email, message, lastUpdate }>`
- `package.json` — Already has `@liveblocks/client@2.20.0`, `@liveblocks/react@2.20.0`, `@liveblocks/node@2.20.0`

The build succeeds with `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=test npm run build`. No dev servers are running. The verifier can start the production server with `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID npm run build && NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID=$ZEALT_RUN_ID npm run start`.
